title: Course Information
metaDescription: Rosehill College course information for Years 9–13 — subject options, NCEA course requirements and option booklets to plan your pathway.

sections.heading: Course information
sections.lede: At Rosehill College, we believe in providing a balanced education that not only focuses on academic excellence but also fosters personal growth and development. Our courses are structured to challenge students intellectually while supporting their individual learning needs.

image.alt: Rosehill College students in school uniform

sections.heading: Finding the right pathway

image.alt: Rosehill College students working in a science class

sections.html:
Here, you will find detailed descriptions of the diverse range of courses we offer, designed to cater to the varied interests and aspirations of our students. Our curriculum is comprehensive and inclusive, ensuring that every student has the opportunity to pursue their passions and achieve their academic goals.

  • Do you need assistance in identifying a pathway?
  • Are you uncertain about the course requirements for your university or trade?
  • Are you feeling overwhelmed by the options available?

Visit our careers advisor at their office, located in the library. Alternatively, you may email them at careers@rosehillcollege.school.nz.

sections.heading: Offered courses
sections.html: At Rosehill College, we strive to create a dynamic and supportive learning environment where junior students can thrive academically, socially, and personally. We are also committed to providing a rich and diverse educational experience that equips our senior students with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to succeed in their future endeavours. Our experienced and dedicated teachers are committed to guiding students through these formative years, ensuring they are well-prepared for next steps in their academic and professional journeys.

sections.heading: Junior courses (Year 9–11)
sections.lede: Our junior curriculum is designed to provide a comprehensive and engaging educational experience for Year 9, Year 10 and Year 11 students. We aim to lay a solid foundation for future academic success and personal growth by offering a wide variety of subjects that cater to diverse interests and abilities.

sections.heading: Senior courses (Year 12 and 13)
sections.lede: Our senior curriculum is designed to provide Year 12 and Year 13 students with specialised and advanced learning opportunities that prepare them for higher education, vocational training, and successful careers. We offer a broad range of subjects, allowing students to tailor their education to their interests and aspirations.

sections.heading: Need help choosing your courses?
sections.text: Our careers advisor is based in the library and can help you identify a pathway, check university or trade requirements, and make sense of the options.
